remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: Spontaneous remission by adulthood: 30-70% symptom reduction (depending on definition); functionally impairing ADHD persists into adulthood in 50-65% of childhood cases; DSMIV criteria met in adulthood in ~50%
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: Stimulant medications (methylphenidate, amphetamines): 60-80% response rate; MTA study: combined medication + behavioural treatment superior to either alone; behavioural interventions alone: 30-50% symptom reduction in children; non-stimulant (atomoxetine, viloxazine): 40-60% response
remission.gap_size: Moderate — substantial underdiagnosis especially in girls/women and adults; treatment gaps exist but pharmacotherapy is highly effective when accessed
remission.barrier_detail: Stimulant shortages (2022-present in US) have left many patients without medication; adult ADHD massively underdiagnosed for decades; medication diversion concerns create barriers to legitimate access; non-pharmacological approaches (CBT, exercise, dietary interventions) chronically under-supported
remission.last_soc_change: 2023 — viloxazine (Qelbree) extended-release approved for adults; 2021 serdexmethylphenidate (Azstarys) approved; 2019 lisdexamfetamine approved for adults and children; methylphenidate/amphetamine backbone essentially unchanged since 1955-1960
remission.outdated_treatments: Pemoline (withdrawn 1999 for hepatotoxicity; residual use); routine ECG screening before stimulant initiation in children without cardiac history (not guideline-recommended but widely practiced); 'drug holidays' without individualized assessment (may impair academic/occupational performance when erroneously applied)
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Exercise (25-30% improvement in executive function); dietary interventions (omega-3, elimination of artificial colours in sensitive children); neurofeedback (modest effect, expensive — best for medication-intolerant patients); working memory training (Cogmed — evidence for generalisation debated); mindfulness-based interventions
remission.frontier_signal: Dasotraline (DRD+SERT inhibitor — Phase 3 data; different mechanism from classic stimulants); centanafadine (triple reuptake inhibitor); digital therapeutics (EndeavorRx — FDA cleared 2020, gamified attention training); cognitive enhancers targeting nicotinic receptor pathways
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.

burden.note: Tier from NIH FY funding ÷ US DALYs (or mortality proxy) vs corpus quartiles
